description: Memory dump cridex.vmem from a Windows XP SP2 machine infected with the Cridex banking trojan (Feodo/Bugat, predecessor of Dridex). The malware injects code into explorer.exe and communicates with C&C servers to steal banking credentials.
tier_note: Evidencia digital no verificable en este momento. No usar hasta obtener hashes canónicos.

artifacts.content: PID 1640: reader_sl.exe | Parent: explorer.exe (PID 1484) | Anomaly: Process hidden from pslist but visible in psscan/psxview | explorer.exe (PID 1464) parent does not appear in pslist | Second unusual wuauclt.exe process | System: Windows XP SP2 x86
forensic_anomalies: reader_sl.exe hidden from the standard process list (psxview discrepancy)
Parent process ID of explorer.exe (1464) does not appear in pslist/psscan, indicating process hollowing
Multiple outbound connections to IPs in high-risk countries

artifacts.content: Active C&C connections from explorer.exe (injected process) | VAD strings show list of targeted financial institutions | Web injection code: HTML fragments from banking pages | Search for 'password' strings in memory reveals captured credentials
forensic_anomalies: Web injection code in explorer.exe memory targeting banking pages
Credential strings found in memory of injected process

artifacts.content: reader_sl.exe simulates Adobe Reader Speed Launcher | Code injection detected via malfind in explorer.exe | Multiple injected DLLs | The legitimate reader_sl.exe from Adobe has different behavior
forensic_anomalies: Masquerading: malware uses a legitimate Adobe process name
Code injection into explorer.exe detected by malfind
